texts.full_text: sufficient to configure with kubernetes? Can someone tell me what are the value for 1. issuer url 2. refreSure, I can help you understand how these values work and provide some guidance on them. ### Issuer URL (issuer_url) The `issuer-url` is a crucial piece of information that identifies where your OpenID Connect provider's public keys are located. This value should point to the endpoint from which Kubernetes will retrieve signing certificates, typically known as JWKS or JSON Web Key Set. - Example: If you're using Google Cloud Platform (GCP) for OIDC authentication and have created a service account that acts like an identity provider, then your issuer URL might look something similar to this: https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v4/certs ``` ### Refresh Token (refresh_token) The `client-secret` is not typically used with refresh tokens in OIDC. Instead of using a client secret for each request, you use the access token that's issued when authentication occurs and then periodically update it via this "Refresh" process. - Example: The value here would be your actual Refresh Token provided by OpenId Connect Provider after successful login. ``` <your_refresh_token> ``` ### Client ID (client_id) This is the unique identifier assigned to you when setting up an application on a service that supports OAuth 2.0, such as Google Cloud Platform or Azure Active Directory. - Example: This value would be something like `YOUR_CLIENT_ID` provided by your OIDC provider during authentication setup. ### Client Secret (client_secret) This is the confidential information used to authenticate and authorize a client with an authorization server when

texts.full_text: in cardigans became common following crosses with (now extinct) red and brindle herding dogs which had more upright ear carriage. Even so, the older types of ears will turn up from time to time and different sorts of ear carriage can even occur in the same litter. These three cardigans are littermates. The one in the middle is a 'fluffy' and his ear type is often seen with the longer coat. Many cardigan puppy's ears will naturally be soft and not stand up until they are between 12-16 weeks old or even a little longer. Don't give up if ears don't stay up at first after the tape is removed. Put the tape on for a few days, then let the ears rest for a few days and repeat again if they still aren't up.
